results.title: Thermal Stress Melatonin Surge in Symbiodiniaceae / NPQ Enhancement
results.novelty_search_result: NOVEL -- zero PubMed papers for 'melatonin AND coral bleaching' or 'melatonin AND Symbiodiniaceae thermal stress'. Confirmed through 2026.
key_strengths: Directly builds on Roopin 2013 verified NPQ enhancement finding
PRJNA723630 dataset provides immediate bioinformatic testability
key_weaknesses: VDE (plant) to DDE (dinoflagellate) cross-kingdom analogy gap -- melatonin never shown to enhance DDE
Antolin 1997 melatonin elevation is cold-shock, not heat-shock -- thermal direction extrapolation
Durusdinium vs Cladocopium melatonin comparison is purely speculative (no data exists)
results.verdict_reason: Novel connection with grounded NPQ enhancement (Roopin 2013). Mechanism plausible but VDE-to-DDE and cold-to-heat extrapolations are substantive gaps. No fabricated claims. Merits experimental testing.

results.title: Dark Priming / SNAT Biomarker -- Nocturnal Melatonin Failure Under Nighttime Warming
results.novelty_search_result: PARTIALLY NOVEL -- Molecular mechanism (melatonin depletion mediating nighttime warming vulnerability) is novel. Ecological prediction (nighttime SST correlates with bleaching) is NOT novel -- NOAA DHW already uses nighttime SST.
key_strengths: SNAT/AANAT bioinformatic prediction immediately executable on PRJNA723630
Honest counter-evidence section acknowledges DHW night temperatures
key_weaknesses: Flagship prediction (nighttime SST > daytime SST for bleaching) already standard NOAA methodology
Dark-adapted cells have low photosynthetic ROS; mitochondrial ROS at 28C insufficient for melatonin depletion
Photocycle-dependent peak in low-light Durusdinium habitat creates internal contradiction
Antolin 1997 showed compensatory melatonin INCREASE under stress, contradicting depletion model
results.verdict_reason: SNAT/AANAT bioinformatic angle is genuinely novel and testable. But ecological prediction is not novel (NOAA already uses nighttime SST). Dark depletion mechanism is weakly supported. Passes on molecular novelty; compromised on ecological prediction novelty.

results.title: Melatonin-AFMK-AMK Cascade as Thermal PSII Shield / GSH Crossover
results.novelty_search_result: NOVEL -- zero results for 'AFMK dinoflagellate' or 'AFMK Symbiodinium'. Cascade concept never applied to coral symbiont photoprotection.
key_strengths: Highly specific quantitative mechanism with rate constants and stoichiometry
AFMK/AMK in dinoflagellates is genuinely novel (zero papers)
Counter-evidence section is unusually honest about GSH competition and 1O2 dominance
Testable metabolite prediction (AFMK/AMK accumulation during thermal ramp)
key_weaknesses: CRITICAL: Quantitative argument built on OH kinetics (k=10^10) but dominant chloroplast ROS is 1O2 (k=10^7, ~500x lower)
215 uM concentration error from Antolin 1997 noted by Critic but not corrected in hypothesis text
GSH at 5mM outcompetes melatonin by >23000:1 for OH -- cascade multiplication insufficient
x10 cascade factor is theoretical maximum; in vivo completion unlikely with competing enzymes
AFMK/AMK never measured in any dinoflagellate -- entire cascade in this organism is theoretical
results.verdict_reason: Novel concept with honest quantitative framework, but core quantitative argument uses wrong ROS species (OH vs 1O2). Concentration error uncorrected. Passes as CONDITIONAL due to genuine novelty and testable predictions, but mechanism is the weakest of the three.
